1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم اقترب للناس حسابهم وهم في غفلة معرضون
1.english: [The time of] their account has approached for the people, while they are in heedlessness turning away.
1.transliteration: Iqtaraba linnaasi hisaa buhum wa hum fee ghaflatim mu'ridoon

1.arabic: ما يأتيهم من ذكر من ربهم محدث إلا استمعوه وهم يلعبون
1.english: No mention comes to them anew from their Lord except that they listen to it while they are at play
1.transliteration: Maa yaateehim min zikrim mir Rabbihim muhdasin illas tama'oohu wa hum yal'aboon

1.arabic: لاهية قلوبهم ۗ وأسروا النجوى الذين ظلموا هل هذا إلا بشر مثلكم ۖ أفتأتون السحر وأنتم تبصرون
1.english: With their hearts distracted. And those who do wrong conceal their private conversation, [saying], "Is this [Prophet] except a human being like you? So would you approach magic while you are aware [of it]?"
1.transliteration: Laahiyatan quloobuhum; wa asarrun najwal lazeena zalamoo hal haazaa illaa basharum mislukum afataa toonas sihra wa antum tubsiroon

1.arabic: قال ربي يعلم القول في السماء والأرض ۖ وهو السميع العليم
1.english: The Prophet said, "My Lord knows whatever is said throughout the heaven and earth, and He is the Hearing, the Knowing."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbee ya'lamul qawla fis samaaa'i wal ardi wa Huwas Samee'ul Aleem

1.arabic: بل قالوا أضغاث أحلام بل افتراه بل هو شاعر فليأتنا بآية كما أرسل الأولون
1.english: But they say, "[The revelation is but] a mixture of false dreams; rather, he has invented it; rather, he is a poet. So let him bring us a sign just as the previous [messengers] were sent [with miracles]."
1.transliteration: Bal qaalooo adghaasu ahlaamim balif taraahu bal huwa shaa'irun falyaatinaa bi Aayatin kamaa ursilal awwaloon

1.arabic: ما آمنت قبلهم من قرية أهلكناها ۖ أفهم يؤمنون
1.english: Not a [single] city which We destroyed believed before them, so will they believe?
1.transliteration: Maaa aaamanat qablahum min qaryatin ahlaknaahaa a-fahum yu'minoon

1.arabic: وما أرسلنا قبلك إلا رجالا نوحي إليهم ۖ فاسألوا أهل الذكر إن كنتم لا تعلمون
1.english: And We sent not before you, [O Muhammad], except men to whom We revealed [the message], so ask the people of the message if you do not know.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa arsalnaa qablaka illaa rijaalan nooheee ilaihim fas'aloo ahlaz zikri in kuntum laa ta'lamoon

1.arabic: وما جعلناهم جسدا لا يأكلون الطعام وما كانوا خالدين
1.english: And We did not make the prophets forms not eating food, nor were they immortal [on earth].
1.transliteration: Wa maa ja'alnaahum jasadal laa yaakuloonat ta'aama wa maa kaanoo khaalideen

1.arabic: ثم صدقناهم الوعد فأنجيناهم ومن نشاء وأهلكنا المسرفين
1.english: Then We fulfilled for them the promise, and We saved them and whom We willed and destroyed the transgressors.
1.transliteration: summa sadaqnaa humul wa'da fa-anjainaahum wa man nashaaa'u wa ahlaknal musrifeen

1.arabic: لقد أنزلنا إليكم كتابا فيه ذكركم ۖ أفلا تعقلون
1.english: We have certainly sent down to you a Book in which is your mention. Then will you not reason?
1.transliteration: Laqad anzalnaaa ilaikum Kitaaban feehi zikrukum afalaa ta'qiloon

1.arabic: وكم قصمنا من قرية كانت ظالمة وأنشأنا بعدها قوما آخرين
1.english: And how many a city which was unjust have We shattered and produced after it another people.
1.transliteration: Wa kam qasamnaa min qaryatin kannat zaalimatanw wa anshadnaa ba'dahaa qawman aakhareen

1.arabic: فلما أحسوا بأسنا إذا هم منها يركضون
1.english: And when its inhabitants perceived Our punishment, at once they fled from it.
1.transliteration: Falammaaa ahassoo baasanaaa izaaa hum minhaa yarkudoon

1.arabic: لا تركضوا وارجعوا إلى ما أترفتم فيه ومساكنكم لعلكم تسألون
1.english: [Some angels said], "Do not flee but return to where you were given luxury and to your homes - perhaps you will be questioned."
1.transliteration: Laa tarkudoo warji'ooo ilaa maaa utriftum feehe wa masaakinikum la'allakum tus'aloon

1.arabic: قالوا يا ويلنا إنا كنا ظالمين
1.english: They said, "O woe to us! Indeed, we were wrongdoers."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo yaa wailanaaa innaa kunnaa zaalimeen

1.arabic: فما زالت تلك دعواهم حتى جعلناهم حصيدا خامدين
1.english: And that declaration of theirs did not cease until We made them [as] a harvest [mowed down], extinguished [like a fire].
1.transliteration: Famaa zaalat tilka da'waahum hattaa ja'alnaahum haseedan khaamideen

1.arabic: وما خلقنا السماء والأرض وما بينهما لاعبين
1.english: And We did not create the heaven and earth and that between them in play.
1.transliteration: Wa maa khalaqnas samaaa'a wal arda wa maa bainahumaa laa'ibeen

1.arabic: لو أردنا أن نتخذ لهوا لاتخذناه من لدنا إن كنا فاعلين
1.english: Had We intended to take a diversion, We could have taken it from [what is] with Us - if [indeed] We were to do so.
1.transliteration: Law aradnaaa an nattakhiza lahwal lat takhaznaahu mil ladunnaaa in kunnaa faa'ileen

1.arabic: بل نقذف بالحق على الباطل فيدمغه فإذا هو زاهق ۚ ولكم الويل مما تصفون
1.english: Rather, We dash the truth upon falsehood, and it destroys it, and thereupon it departs. And for you is destruction from that which you describe.
1.transliteration: Bal naqzifu bilhaqqi 'alal baatili fa yadmaghuhoo fa izaa huwa zaahiq; wa lakumul wailu mimmaa tasifoon

1.arabic: وله من في السماوات والأرض ۚ ومن عنده لا يستكبرون عن عبادته ولا يستحسرون
1.english: To Him belongs whoever is in the heavens and the earth. And those near Him are not prevented by arrogance from His worship, nor do they tire.
1.transliteration: Wa lahoo man fis samaawaati wal ard; wa man 'indahoo laa yastakbiroona 'an 'ibaada tihee wa laa yastahsiroon

1.arabic: يسبحون الليل والنهار لا يفترون
1.english: They exalt [Him] night and day [and] do not slacken.
1.transliteration: Yusabbihoona laila wannahaara laa yafturoon

1.arabic: أم اتخذوا آلهة من الأرض هم ينشرون
1.english: Or have men taken for themselves gods from the earth who resurrect [the dead]?
1.transliteration: Amit takhazooo aalihatam minal ardi hum yunshiroon

1.arabic: لو كان فيهما آلهة إلا الله لفسدتا ۚ فسبحان الله رب العرش عما يصفون
1.english: Had there been within the heavens and earth gods besides Allah, they both would have been ruined. So exalted is Allah, Lord of the Throne, above what they describe.
1.transliteration: Law kaana feehimaaa aalihatun illal laahu lafasadataa; fa-Subhaanal laahi Rabbil 'Arshi 'ammaa yasifoon

1.arabic: لا يسأل عما يفعل وهم يسألون
1.english: He is not questioned about what He does, but they will be questioned.
1.transliteration: Laa yus'alu 'ammaa yaf'alu wa hum yus'aloon

1.arabic: أم اتخذوا من دونه آلهة ۖ قل هاتوا برهانكم ۖ هذا ذكر من معي وذكر من قبلي ۗ بل أكثرهم لا يعلمون الحق ۖ فهم معرضون
1.english: Or have they taken gods besides Him? Say, [O Muhammad], "Produce your proof. This [Qur'an] is the message for those with me and the message of those before me." But most of them do not know the truth, so they are turning away.
1.transliteration: Amit takhazoo min doonihee aalihatan qul haatoo burhaanakum haaza zikru mam ma'iya wa zikru man qablee; bal aksaruhum laa ya'lamoonal haqqa fahum mu'ridoon

1.arabic: وما أرسلنا من قبلك من رسول إلا نوحي إليه أنه لا إله إلا أنا فاعبدون
1.english: And We sent not before you any messenger except that We revealed to him that, "There is no deity except Me, so worship Me."
1.transliteration: Wa maaa arsalnaa min qablika mir Rasoolin illaa nooheee ilaihi annahoo laaa ilaaha illaaa Ana fa'budoon

1.arabic: وقالوا اتخذ الرحمن ولدا ۗ سبحانه ۚ بل عباد مكرمون
1.english: And they say, "The Most Merciful has taken a son." Exalted is He! Rather, they are [but] honored servants.
1.transliteration: Wa qaalut takhazar Rahmaanu waladan Subhaanah; bal 'ibaadum mkkramoon

1.arabic: لا يسبقونه بالقول وهم بأمره يعملون
1.english: They cannot precede Him in word, and they act by His command.
1.transliteration: Laa yasbiqoonahoo bil qawli wa hum bi amrihee ya'maloon

1.arabic: يعلم ما بين أيديهم وما خلفهم ولا يشفعون إلا لمن ارتضى وهم من خشيته مشفقون
1.english: He knows what is [presently] before them and what will be after them, and they cannot intercede except on behalf of one whom He approves. And they, from fear of Him, are apprehensive.
1.transliteration: Ya'lamu maa baina aideehim wa maa khalfahum wa laa yashfa'oona illaa limanir tadaa wa hum min khash yatihee mushfiqoon

1.arabic: ۞ ومن يقل منهم إني إله من دونه فذلك نجزيه جهنم ۚ كذلك نجزي الظالمين
1.english: And whoever of them should say, "Indeed, I am a god besides Him"- that one We would recompense with Hell. Thus do We recompense the wrongdoers.
1.transliteration: Wa mai yaqul minhum inneee ilaahum min doonihee fazaalika najzeehi Jahannam; kazaalika najziz zaalimeen

1.arabic: أولم ير الذين كفروا أن السماوات والأرض كانتا رتقا ففتقناهما ۖ وجعلنا من الماء كل شيء حي ۖ أفلا يؤمنون
1.english: Have those who disbelieved not considered that the heavens and the earth were a joined entity, and We separated them and made from water every living thing? Then will they not believe?
1.transliteration: Awalam yaral lazeena kafarooo annas samaawaati wal arda kaanataa ratqan faftaqnaahumaa wa ja'alnaa minal maaa'i kulla shai'in haiyin afalaa yu'minoon

1.arabic: وجعلنا في الأرض رواسي أن تميد بهم وجعلنا فيها فجاجا سبلا لعلهم يهتدون
1.english: And We placed within the earth firmly set mountains, lest it should shift with them, and We made therein [mountain] passes [as] roads that they might be guided.
1.transliteration: Wa ja'alnaa fil ardi rawaasiya an tameeda bihim wa ja'alnaa feehaa fijaajan subulal la'allahum yahtadoon

1.arabic: وجعلنا السماء سقفا محفوظا ۖ وهم عن آياتها معرضون
1.english: And We made the sky a protected ceiling, but they, from its signs, are turning away.
1.transliteration: Wa ja'alnas samaaa'a saqfam mahfoozanw wa hum 'an Aayaatihaa mu'ridoon

1.arabic: وهو الذي خلق الليل والنهار والشمس والقمر ۖ كل في فلك يسبحون
1.english: And it is He who created the night and the day and the sun and the moon; all [heavenly bodies] in an orbit are swimming.
1.transliteration: Wa Huwal lazee khalaqal laila wannahaara washshamsa wal qamara kullun fee falakiny yashbahoon

1.arabic: وما جعلنا لبشر من قبلك الخلد ۖ أفإن مت فهم الخالدون
1.english: And We did not grant to any man before you eternity [on earth]; so if you die - would they be eternal?
1.transliteration: Wa maa ja'alnaa libasharim min qablikal khuld; afaimmitta fahumul khaalidoon

1.arabic: كل نفس ذائقة الموت ۗ ونبلوكم بالشر والخير فتنة ۖ وإلينا ترجعون
1.english: Every soul will taste death. And We test you with evil and with good as trial; and to Us you will be returned.
1.transliteration: Kullu nafsin zaaa'iqatul mawt; wa nablookum bishsharri walkhairi fitnatanw wa ilainaa turja'oon

1.arabic: وإذا رآك الذين كفروا إن يتخذونك إلا هزوا أهذا الذي يذكر آلهتكم وهم بذكر الرحمن هم كافرون
1.english: And when those who disbelieve see you, [O Muhammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one who insults your gods?" And they are, at the mention of the Most Merciful, disbelievers.
1.transliteration: Wa izaa ra aakal lazeena kafarooo iny-yattakhizoonaka illa huzuwaa; ahaazal lazee yazkuru aalihatakum wa hum bi zikrir Rahmaani hum kaafiroon

1.arabic: خلق الإنسان من عجل ۚ سأريكم آياتي فلا تستعجلون
1.english: Man was created of haste. I will show you My signs, so do not impatiently urge Me.
1.transliteration: Khuliqal insaanu min 'ajal; sa ureekum Aayaatee falaa tasta'jiloon

1.arabic: ويقولون متى هذا الوعد إن كنتم صادقين
1.english: And they say, "When is this promise, if you should be truthful?"
1.transliteration: Wa yaqooloona mataa haazal wa'du in kuntum saadiqeen

1.arabic: لو يعلم الذين كفروا حين لا يكفون عن وجوههم النار ولا عن ظهورهم ولا هم ينصرون
1.english: If those who disbelieved but knew the time when they will not avert the Fire from their faces or from their backs and they will not be aided...
1.transliteration: Law ya'lamul lazeena kafaroo heena laa yakuffoona 'anw wujoohihimun Naara wa laa 'an zuhoorihim wa laa hum yunsaroon

1.arabic: بل تأتيهم بغتة فتبهتهم فلا يستطيعون ردها ولا هم ينظرون
1.english: Rather, it will come to them unexpectedly and bewilder them, and they will not be able to repel it, nor will they be reprieved.
1.transliteration: Bal taateehim baghtatan fatabhatuhum falaa yastatee'oona raddahaa wa laa hum yunzaroon

1.arabic: ولقد استهزئ برسل من قبلك فحاق بالذين سخروا منهم ما كانوا به يستهزئون
1.english: And already were messengers ridiculed before you, but those who mocked them were enveloped by what they used to ridicule.
1.transliteration: Wa laqadis tuhzi'a bi-Rusulim min qablika fahaaqa billazeena sakhiroo minhum maa kaanoo bihee yastahzi'oon

1.arabic: قل من يكلؤكم بالليل والنهار من الرحمن ۗ بل هم عن ذكر ربهم معرضون
1.english: Say, "Who can protect you at night or by day from the Most Merciful?" But they are, from the remembrance of their Lord, turning away.
1.transliteration: Qul mai yakla 'ukum billaili wannahaari minar Rahmaan; bal hum 'an zikri Rabbihim mu'ridoon

1.arabic: أم لهم آلهة تمنعهم من دوننا ۚ لا يستطيعون نصر أنفسهم ولا هم منا يصحبون
1.english: Or do they have gods to defend them other than Us? They are unable [even] to help themselves, nor can they be protected from Us.
1.transliteration: Am lahum aalihatun tamna'ulum min dooninaa; laa yastatee'oona nasra anfusihim wa laa hum minnna yus-haboon

1.arabic: بل متعنا هؤلاء وآباءهم حتى طال عليهم العمر ۗ أفلا يرون أنا نأتي الأرض ننقصها من أطرافها ۚ أفهم الغالبون
1.english: But, [on the contrary], We have provided good things for these [disbelievers] and their fathers until life was prolonged for them. Then do they not see that We set upon the land, reducing it from its borders? So it is they who will overcome?
1.transliteration: Bal matta'naa haaa'ulaaa'i wa aabaaa'ahum hattaa taala 'alaihimul 'umur; afalaa yarawna anna naatil arda nanqusuhaa min atraafihaa; afahumul ghaaliboon

1.arabic: قل إنما أنذركم بالوحي ۚ ولا يسمع الصم الدعاء إذا ما ينذرون
1.english: Say, "I only warn you by revelation." But the deaf do not hear the call when they are warned.
1.transliteration: Qul innamaaa unzirukum bilwahyi; wa laa yasma'us summud du'aaa'a izaa maa yunzaroon

1.arabic: ولئن مستهم نفحة من عذاب ربك ليقولن يا ويلنا إنا كنا ظالمين
1.english: And if [as much as] a whiff of the punishment of your Lord should touch them, they would surely say, "O woe to us! Indeed, we have been wrongdoers."
1.transliteration: Wa la'im massat hum nafhatum min 'azaabi Rabbika la yaqoolunna yaawailanaaa innnaa kunnaa zaalimeen

1.arabic: ونضع الموازين القسط ليوم القيامة فلا تظلم نفس شيئا ۖ وإن كان مثقال حبة من خردل أتينا بها ۗ وكفى بنا حاسبين
1.english: And We place the scales of justice for the Day of Resurrection, so no soul will be treated unjustly at all. And if there is [even] the weight of a mustard seed, We will bring it forth. And sufficient are We as accountant.
1.transliteration: Wa nada'ul mawaazeenal qista li Yawmil Qiyaamati falaa tuzlamu nafsun shai'aa; wa in kaana misqaala habbatim min khardalin atainaa bihaa; wa kafaa binaa haasibeen

1.arabic: ولقد آتينا موسى وهارون الفرقان وضياء وذكرا للمتقين
1.english: And We had already given Moses and Aaron the criterion and a light and a reminder for the righteous
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aatainaa Moosa wa haaroonal Furqaana wa diyaa'anw wa zikral lilmuttaqeen

1.arabic: الذين يخشون ربهم بالغيب وهم من الساعة مشفقون
1.english: Who fear their Lord unseen, while they are of the Hour apprehensive.
1.transliteration: Allazeena yakhshawna Rabbahum bilghaibi wa hum minas Saa'ati mushfiqoon

1.arabic: وهذا ذكر مبارك أنزلناه ۚ أفأنتم له منكرون
1.english: And this [Qur'an] is a blessed message which We have sent down. Then are you with it unacquainted?
1.transliteration: Wa haazaa Zikrum Mubaarakun anzalnaah; afa antum lahoo munkiroon

1.arabic: ۞ ولقد آتينا إبراهيم رشده من قبل وكنا به عالمين
1.english: And We had certainly given Abraham his sound judgement before, and We were of him well-Knowing
1.transliteration: Wa laqad aatainaaa Ibraaheema rushdahoo min qablu wa kunnaa bihee 'aalimeen

1.arabic: إذ قال لأبيه وقومه ما هذه التماثيل التي أنتم لها عاكفون
1.english: When he said to his father and his people, "What are these statues to which you are devoted?"
1.transliteration: Iz qaala li abeehi wa qawmihee maa haazihit tamaaseelul lateee antum lahee 'aakifoon

1.arabic: قالوا وجدنا آباءنا لها عابدين
1.english: They said, "We found our fathers worshippers of them."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo wajadnaaa aabaaa'anaa lahaa 'aabideen

1.arabic: قال لقد كنتم أنتم وآباؤكم في ضلال مبين
1.english: He said, "You were certainly, you and your fathers, in manifest error."
1.transliteration: Qaala laqad kuntum antum wa aabaaa'ukum fee dalaalim mubeen

1.arabic: قالوا أجئتنا بالحق أم أنت من اللاعبين
1.english: They said, "Have you come to us with truth, or are you of those who jest?"
1.transliteration: Qaalooo aji'tanaa bil haqqi am anta minal laa'ibeen

1.arabic: قال بل ربكم رب السماوات والأرض الذي فطرهن وأنا على ذلكم من الشاهدين
1.english: He said, "[No], rather, your Lord is the Lord of the heavens and the earth who created them, and I, to that, am of those who testify.
1.transliteration: Qaala bar Rabbukum Rabbus samaawaati wal ardil lazee fatarahunna wa ana 'alaa zaalikum minash shaahideen

1.arabic: وتالله لأكيدن أصنامكم بعد أن تولوا مدبرين
1.english: And [I swear] by Allah, I will surely plan against your idols after you have turned and gone away."
1.transliteration: Wa tallaahi la akeedanna asnaamakum ba'da an tuwalloo mudbireen

1.arabic: فجعلهم جذاذا إلا كبيرا لهم لعلهم إليه يرجعون
1.english: So he made them into fragments, except a large one among them, that they might return to it [and question].
1.transliteration: Faja'alahum juzaazan illaa kabeeral lahum la'allahum ilaihi yarji'oon

1.arabic: قالوا من فعل هذا بآلهتنا إنه لمن الظالمين
1.english: They said, "Who has done this to our gods? Indeed, he is of the wrongdoers."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo man fa'ala haazaa bi aalihatinaaa innahoo laminaz zaalimeen

1.arabic: قالوا سمعنا فتى يذكرهم يقال له إبراهيم
1.english: They said, "We heard a young man mention them who is called Abraham."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo sami'naa fatany yazkuruhum yuqaalu lahooo Ibraaheem

1.arabic: قالوا فأتوا به على أعين الناس لعلهم يشهدون
1.english: They said, "Then bring him before the eyes of the people that they may testify."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo faatoo bihee 'alaaa a'yunin naasi la'allahum yash hadoon

1.arabic: قالوا أأنت فعلت هذا بآلهتنا يا إبراهيم
1.english: They said, "Have you done this to our gods, O Abraham?"
1.transliteration: Qaalooo 'a-anta fa'alta haazaa bi aalihatinaa yaaa Ibraaheem

1.arabic: قال بل فعله كبيرهم هذا فاسألوهم إن كانوا ينطقون
1.english: He said, "Rather, this - the largest of them - did it, so ask them, if they should [be able to] speak."
1.transliteration: Qaala bal fa'alahoo kabeeruhum haazaa fas'aloohum in kaanoo yantiqoon

1.arabic: فرجعوا إلى أنفسهم فقالوا إنكم أنتم الظالمون
1.english: So they returned to [blaming] themselves and said [to each other], "Indeed, you are the wrongdoers."
1.transliteration: Faraja'ooo ilaaa anfusihim faqaalooo innakum antumuz zaalimoon

1.arabic: ثم نكسوا على رءوسهم لقد علمت ما هؤلاء ينطقون
1.english: Then they reversed themselves, [saying], "You have already known that these do not speak!"
1.transliteration: Summa nukisoo 'alaa ru'oosihim laqad 'alimta maa haaa'ulaaa'i yantiqoon

1.arabic: قال أفتعبدون من دون الله ما لا ينفعكم شيئا ولا يضركم
1.english: He said, "Then do you worship instead of Allah that which does not benefit you at all or harm you?
1.transliteration: Qaala afata'budoona min doonil laahi maa laa yanfa'ukum shai'anw wa laa yadurrukum

1.arabic: أف لكم ولما تعبدون من دون الله ۖ أفلا تعقلون
1.english: Uff to you and to what you worship instead of Allah. Then will you not use reason?"
1.transliteration: Uffil lakum wa limaa ta'budoona min doonil laah; afalaa ta'qiloon

1.arabic: قالوا حرقوه وانصروا آلهتكم إن كنتم فاعلين
1.english: They said, "Burn him and support your gods - if you are to act."
1.transliteration: Qaaloo harriqooho wansurooo aalihatakum in kuntum faa'ileen

1.arabic: قلنا يا نار كوني بردا وسلاما على إبراهيم
1.english: Allah said, "O fire, be coolness and safety upon Abraham."
1.transliteration: Qulnaa yaa naaru koonee bardanw wa salaaman 'alaaa Ibraaheem

1.arabic: وأرادوا به كيدا فجعلناهم الأخسرين
1.english: And they intended for him harm, but We made them the greatest losers.
1.transliteration: Wa araadoo bihee kaidan faja'alnaahumul akhsareen

1.arabic: ونجيناه ولوطا إلى الأرض التي باركنا فيها للعالمين
1.english: And We delivered him and Lot to the land which We had blessed for the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa najjainaahu wa Lootan ilal ardil latee baaraknaa feehaa lil 'aalameen

1.arabic: ووهبنا له إسحاق ويعقوب نافلة ۖ وكلا جعلنا صالحين
1.english: And We gave him Isaac and Jacob in addition, and all [of them] We made righteous.
1.transliteration: Wa wahabnaa lahooo Ishaaq; wa Ya'qooba naafilah; wa kullan ja'alnaa saaliheen

1.arabic: وجعلناهم أئمة يهدون بأمرنا وأوحينا إليهم فعل الخيرات وإقام الصلاة وإيتاء الزكاة ۖ وكانوا لنا عابدين
1.english: And We made them leaders guiding by Our command. And We inspired to them the doing of good deeds, establishment of prayer, and giving of zakah; and they were worshippers of Us.
1.transliteration: Wa ja'alnaahum a'immatany yahdoona bi amrinaa wa awhainaaa ilaihim fi'lal khairaati wa iqaamas Salaati wa eetaaa'az Zakaati wa kaanoo lanaa 'aabideen

1.arabic: ولوطا آتيناه حكما وعلما ونجيناه من القرية التي كانت تعمل الخبائث ۗ إنهم كانوا قوم سوء فاسقين
1.english: And to Lot We gave judgement and knowledge, and We saved him from the city that was committing wicked deeds. Indeed, they were a people of evil, defiantly disobedient.
1.transliteration: Wa Lootan aatainaahu hukmanw wa 'ilmanw wa najjainaahu minal qaryatil latee kaanat ta'malul khabaaa'is; innahum kaanoo qawma saw'in faasiqeen

1.arabic: وأدخلناه في رحمتنا ۖ إنه من الصالحين
1.english: And We admitted him into Our mercy. Indeed, he was of the righteous.
1.transliteration: Wa adkhalnaahu fee rahmatinaa innahoo minas saalihee

1.arabic: ونوحا إذ نادى من قبل فاستجبنا له فنجيناه وأهله من الكرب العظيم
1.english: And [mention] Noah, when he called [to Allah] before [that time], so We responded to him and saved him and his family from the great flood.
1.transliteration: Wa noohan iz naadaa min qablu fastajabnaa lahoo fanajjainaahu wa ahlahoo minal karbil 'azeem

1.arabic: ونصرناه من القوم الذين كذبوا بآياتنا ۚ إنهم كانوا قوم سوء فأغرقناهم أجمعين
1.english: And We saved him from the people who denied Our signs. Indeed, they were a people of evil, so We drowned them, all together.
1.transliteration: Wa nasarnaahu minal qawmil lazeena kazzaboo bi Aayaatinaa; innahum kkaanoo qawma saw'in fa-aghraq naahum ajma'een

1.arabic: وداوود وسليمان إذ يحكمان في الحرث إذ نفشت فيه غنم القوم وكنا لحكمهم شاهدين
1.english: And [mention] David and Solomon, when they judged concerning the field - when the sheep of a people overran it [at night], and We were witness to their judgement.
1.transliteration: Wa Daawooda wa Sulaimaana iz yahkumaani fil harsi iz nafashat feehi ghanamul qawmi wa kunnaa lihukmihim shaahideen

1.arabic: ففهمناها سليمان ۚ وكلا آتينا حكما وعلما ۚ وسخرنا مع داوود الجبال يسبحن والطير ۚ وكنا فاعلين
1.english: And We gave understanding of the case to Solomon, and to each [of them] We gave judgement and knowledge. And We subjected the mountains to exalt [Us], along with David and [also] the birds. And We were doing [that].
1.transliteration: Fafahhamnaahaa sulaimaan; wa kullan aatainaa hukmanw wa'ilmanw wa sakh kharnaa ma'a Daawoodal jibaala yusabbihna wattayr; wa kunnaa faa'ileen

1.arabic: وعلمناه صنعة لبوس لكم لتحصنكم من بأسكم ۖ فهل أنتم شاكرون
1.english: And We taught him the fashioning of coats of armor to protect you from your [enemy in] battle. So will you then be grateful?
1.transliteration: Wa 'allamanaahu san'ata laboosil lakum lituhsinakum mim baasikum fahal antum shaakiroon

1.arabic: ولسليمان الريح عاصفة تجري بأمره إلى الأرض التي باركنا فيها ۚ وكنا بكل شيء عالمين
1.english: And to Solomon [We subjected] the wind, blowing forcefully, proceeding by his command toward the land which We had blessed. And We are ever, of all things, Knowing.
1.transliteration: Wa li Sulaimaanar reeha 'aasifatan tajree bi amriheee ilal ardil latee baaraknaa feehaa; wa kunnaa bikulli shai'in 'aalimeen

1.arabic: ومن الشياطين من يغوصون له ويعملون عملا دون ذلك ۖ وكنا لهم حافظين
1.english: And of the devils were those who dived for him and did work other than that. And We were of them a guardian.
1.transliteration: Wa minash Shayaateeni mai yaghoosoona lahoo wa ya'maloona 'amalan doona zaalika wa kunna lahum baafizeen

1.arabic: ۞ وأيوب إذ نادى ربه أني مسني الضر وأنت أرحم الراحمين
1.english: And [mention] Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, adversity has touched me, and you are the Most Merciful of the merciful."
1.transliteration: Wa Ayyooba iz naadaa Rabbahooo annee massaniyad durru wa Anta arhamur raahimeen

1.arabic: فاستجبنا له فكشفنا ما به من ضر ۖ وآتيناه أهله ومثلهم معهم رحمة من عندنا وذكرى للعابدين
1.english: So We responded to him and removed what afflicted him of adversity. And We gave him [back] his family and the like thereof with them as mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers [of Allah].
1.transliteration: Fastajabnaa lahoo fakashaf naa maa bihee min durrinw wa aatainaahu ahlahoo wa mislahum ma'ahum rahmatam min 'indinaa wa zikraa lil'aabideen

1.arabic: وإسماعيل وإدريس وذا الكفل ۖ كل من الصابرين
1.english: And [mention] Ishmael and Idrees and Dhul-Kifl; all were of the patient.
1.transliteration: Wa Ismaa'eela wa Idreesa wa Zal Kifli kullum minas saabireen

1.arabic: وأدخلناهم في رحمتنا ۖ إنهم من الصالحين
1.english: And We admitted them into Our mercy. Indeed, they were of the righteous.
1.transliteration: Wa adkhalnaahum fee rahmatinaa innahum minas saaliheen

1.arabic: وذا النون إذ ذهب مغاضبا فظن أن لن نقدر عليه فنادى في الظلمات أن لا إله إلا أنت سبحانك إني كنت من الظالمين
1.english: And [mention] the man of the fish, when he went off in anger and thought that We would not decree [anything] upon him. And he called out within the darknesses, "There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers."
1.transliteration: Wa Zan Nooni iz zahaba mughaadiban fazaanna al lan naqdira 'alaihi fanaanna al lan naqdira 'alaihi fanaadaa fiz zulumaati al laaa ilaaha illaaa Anta Subhaanaka innee kuntu minaz zaalimeen

1.arabic: فاستجبنا له ونجيناه من الغم ۚ وكذلك ننجي المؤمنين
1.english: So We responded to him and saved him from the distress. And thus do We save the believers.
1.transliteration: Fastajabnaa lahoo wa najjainaahu minal ghamm; wa kazaalika nunjil mu'mineen

1.arabic: وزكريا إذ نادى ربه رب لا تذرني فردا وأنت خير الوارثين
1.english: And [mention] Zechariah, when he called to his Lord, "My Lord, do not leave me alone [with no heir], while you are the best of inheritors."
1.transliteration: Wa Zakariyyaaa iz naadaa Rabbahoo Rabbi laa tazarnee fardanw wa Anta khairul waariseen

1.arabic: فاستجبنا له ووهبنا له يحيى وأصلحنا له زوجه ۚ إنهم كانوا يسارعون في الخيرات ويدعوننا رغبا ورهبا ۖ وكانوا لنا خاشعين
1.english: So We responded to him, and We gave to him John, and amended for him his wife. Indeed, they used to hasten to good deeds and supplicate Us in hope and fear, and they were to Us humbly submissive.
1.transliteration: Fastajabnaa lahoo wa wahabnaa lahoo Yahyaa Wa aslahnaa lahoo zawjah; innahum kaanoo yusaari'oona fil khairaati wa yad'oonanaa raghabanw wa rahabaa; wa kaanoo lanaa khaashi'een

1.arabic: والتي أحصنت فرجها فنفخنا فيها من روحنا وجعلناها وابنها آية للعالمين
1.english: And [mention] the one who guarded her chastity, so We blew into her [garment] through Our angel [Gabriel], and We made her and her son a sign for the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wallateee ahsanat farjahaa fanafakhnaa feehaa mir roohinaa wa ja'alnaahaa wabnahaaa Aayatal lil'aalameen

1.arabic: إن هذه أمتكم أمة واحدة وأنا ربكم فاعبدون
1.english: Indeed this, your religion, is one religion, and I am your Lord, so worship Me.
1.transliteration: Inna haaziheee ummatukum ummatanw waahidatanw wa Ana Rabbukum fa'budoon

1.arabic: وتقطعوا أمرهم بينهم ۖ كل إلينا راجعون
1.english: And [yet] they divided their affair among themselves, [but] all to Us will return.
1.transliteration: Wa taqatta'ooo amrahum bainahum kullun ilainaaa raaji'oon

1.arabic: فمن يعمل من الصالحات وهو مؤمن فلا كفران لسعيه وإنا له كاتبون
1.english: So whoever does righteous deeds while he is a believer - no denial will there be for his effort, and indeed We, of it, are recorders.
1.transliteration: Famai ya'mal minas saalihaati wa huwa mu'minun falaa kufraana lisa'yihee wa innaa lahoo kaatiboon

1.arabic: وحرام على قرية أهلكناها أنهم لا يرجعون
1.english: And there is prohibition upon [the people of] a city which We have destroyed that they will [ever] return
1.transliteration: Wa haraamun 'alaa qaryatin ahlaknaahaaa annahum laa yarji'oon

1.arabic: حتى إذا فتحت يأجوج ومأجوج وهم من كل حدب ينسلون
1.english: Until when [the dam of] Gog and Magog has been opened and they, from every elevation, descend
1.transliteration: Hattaaa izaa futihat Yaajooju wa Maajooju wa hum min kulli hadabiny yansiloon

1.arabic: واقترب الوعد الحق فإذا هي شاخصة أبصار الذين كفروا يا ويلنا قد كنا في غفلة من هذا بل كنا ظالمين
1.english: And [when] the true promise has approached; then suddenly the eyes of those who disbelieved will be staring [in horror, while they say], "O woe to us; we had been unmindful of this; rather, we were wrongdoers."
1.transliteration: Waqtarabal wa'dul haqqu fa-izaa hiya shaakhisatun absaarul lazeena kafaroo yaawailanaa qad kunna fee ghaflatim min haaza bal kunnaa zaalimeen

1.arabic: إنكم وما تعبدون من دون الله حصب جهنم أنتم لها واردون
1.english: Indeed, you [disbelievers] and what you worship other than Allah are the firewood of Hell. You will be coming to [enter] it.
1.transliteration: Innakum wa maa ta'budoona min doonil laahi hasabu Jahannama antum lahaa waaridoon

1.arabic: لو كان هؤلاء آلهة ما وردوها ۖ وكل فيها خالدون
1.english: Had these [false deities] been [actual] gods, they would not have come to it, but all are eternal therein.
1.transliteration: Law kaana haaa'ulaaa'i aalihatam maa waradoohaa wa kullun feehaa khaalidoon

1.arabic: لهم فيها زفير وهم فيها لا يسمعون
1.english: For them therein is heavy sighing, and they therein will not hear.
1.transliteration: Lahum feehaa zafeerunw wa hum feehaa laa yasma'oon

1.arabic: إن الذين سبقت لهم منا الحسنى أولئك عنها مبعدون
1.english: Indeed, those for whom the best [reward] has preceded from Us - they are from it far removed.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena sabaqat lahum minnal husnaaa ulaaa'ika 'anhaa mub'adoon

1.arabic: لا يسمعون حسيسها ۖ وهم في ما اشتهت أنفسهم خالدون
1.english: They will not hear its sound, while they are, in that which their souls desire, abiding eternally.
1.transliteration: Laa yasma'oona hasee sahaa wa hum fee mash tahat anfusuhum khaalidoon

1.arabic: لا يحزنهم الفزع الأكبر وتتلقاهم الملائكة هذا يومكم الذي كنتم توعدون
1.english: They will not be grieved by the greatest terror, and the angels will meet them, [saying], "This is your Day which you have been promised" -
1.transliteration: Laa yahzunuhumul faza'ul akbaru wa tatalaq qaahumul malaaa'ikatu haazaa Yawmukumul lazee kuntum too'adoon

1.arabic: يوم نطوي السماء كطي السجل للكتب ۚ كما بدأنا أول خلق نعيده ۚ وعدا علينا ۚ إنا كنا فاعلين
1.english: The Day when We will fold the heaven like the folding of a [written] sheet for the records. As We began the first creation, We will repeat it. [That is] a promise binding upon Us. Indeed, We will do it.
1.transliteration: Yawma natwis samaaa'a kataiyis sijilli lilkutub; kamaa badaanaa awwala khalqin nu'eeduh; wa'dan 'alainaa; innaa kunna faa'ileen

1.arabic: ولقد كتبنا في الزبور من بعد الذكر أن الأرض يرثها عبادي الصالحون
1.english: And We have already written in the book [of Psalms] after the [previous] mention that the land [of Paradise] is inherited by My righteous servants.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad katabnaa fiz Zaboori mim ba'diz zikri annal arda yarisuhaa 'ibaadi yas saalihoon

1.arabic: إن في هذا لبلاغا لقوم عابدين
1.english: Indeed, in this [Qur'an] is notification for a worshipping people.
1.transliteration: Inna fee haaza labalaa ghal liqawmin 'aabideen

1.arabic: وما أرسلناك إلا رحمة للعالمين
1.english: And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a mercy to the worlds.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa arsalnaaka illaa rahmatal lil'aalameen

1.arabic: قل إنما يوحى إلي أنما إلهكم إله واحد ۖ فهل أنتم مسلمون
1.english: Say, "It is only revealed to me that your god is but one God; so will you be Muslims [in submission to Him]?"
1.transliteration: Qul innamaa yoohaa ilaiya annamaaa ilaahukum illaahunw Waahid, fahal antum muslimoon

1.arabic: فإن تولوا فقل آذنتكم على سواء ۖ وإن أدري أقريب أم بعيد ما توعدون
1.english: But if they turn away, then say, "I have announced to [all of] you equally. And I know not whether near or far is that which you are promised.
1.transliteration: Fa in tawallaw faqul aazantukum 'alaa sawaaa'; wa in adreee aqareebun am ba'eedum maa too'adoon

1.arabic: إنه يعلم الجهر من القول ويعلم ما تكتمون
1.english: Indeed, He knows what is declared of speech, and He knows what you conceal.
1.transliteration: Innahoo ya'lamul jahra minal qawli wa ya'lamu maa taktumoon

1.arabic: وإن أدري لعله فتنة لكم ومتاع إلى حين
1.english: And I know not; perhaps it is a trial for you and enjoyment for a time."
1.transliteration: Wa in adree la'allahoo fitnatul lakum wa mataa'un ilaaheen

1.arabic: قال رب احكم بالحق ۗ وربنا الرحمن المستعان على ما تصفون
1.english: [The Prophet] has said, "My Lord, judge [between us] in truth. And our Lord is the Most Merciful, the one whose help is sought against that which you describe."
1.transliteration: Qaala Rabbih kum bil haqq; wa Rabbunar Rahmaa nul musta'aanu 'alaa maa tasifoon
